_metadata.source: World Economic Forum. 'The Future of Jobs Report 2025.' January 2025.
_metadata.surveyBase: Future of Jobs Survey 2024, covering 1,000+ employers across 55 economies and 22 sectors.
_metadata.dataSource: International Labour Organization, ILOSTAT; World Economic Forum Global Skills Taxonomy.

technologyTrends.description: Share of employers surveyed that identify the stated technology trend as likely to drive business transformation.
technologyTrends.keyFinding: AI and information processing technologies are expected to have the biggest impact, with 86% of respondents expecting these technologies to transform their business by 2030.

skillsOnTheRise.description: Skills ranked by net increase — the difference between the share of employers that consider a skill to be increasing vs. decreasing in use, 2025–2030.

coreSkills2025._note: Only top 5 of 26 skills included. Figure 3.3 contains all 26 with share percentages. If a comparison visualization pairs core skills against skillsOnTheRise (26 items), the full set should be extracted here.
coreSkills2025.description: Share of employers who consider the stated skills to be core skills for their workforce in 2025.
coreSkills2025.note: Analytical thinking remains the most sought-after core skill, with seven out of 10 companies considering it essential.

skillInstability.description: On average, workers can expect that 39% of their existing skill sets will be transformed or become outdated over the 2025–2030 period, down from 44% in 2023 and 57% in 2020.

globalEmploymentChange.description: 170 million jobs projected to be created, 92 million displaced, constituting 22% structural churn of the 1.2 billion formal jobs in the dataset, amounting to a net 7% increase.

fastestGrowingRoles._note: Net growth percentages are chart-only data in the source (bar chart without labeled values). Manual extraction from the visual would be needed to populate netGrowthPct fields.

roles.role: Light Truck or Delivery Services Drivers

fastestDecliningRoles._note: Net decline percentages are chart-only data in the source. See fastestGrowingRoles._note.

roles.role: Door-To-Door Sales Workers, News and Street Vendors, and Related Workers

transformationBarriers.description: Share of employers surveyed expecting the stated barrier will hinder their organizational transformation, 2025–2030.

barriers.barrier: Skills gaps in the labour market
Organizational culture and resistance to change
Inability to attract talent to the industry
Lack of adequate data and technical infrastructure
Inability to attract talent to my firm

keyFindings: Skills gaps rank first in 52 out of 55 economies and 19 out of 22 sectors.
This is more pronounced than the 2023 edition, where skills gaps were backed by 60% of executives.

generalStrategies.description: Share of employers surveyed planning to implement the stated workforce strategy in response to macrotrends, 2025–2030.

strategies.strategy: Transitioning staff from declining to growing roles
Reducing staff as their skills become less relevant

aiSpecificStrategies.description: Share of employers planning to implement the stated strategy in response to AI's increasing capability and prevalence.

strategies.strategy: Reskilling and upskilling existing workforce to better work alongside AI
Hiring new people with skills to design AI tools and enhancements
Hiring new people with skills to better work alongside AI
Re-orienting organization to target new business opportunities created by AI
Transitioning people from jobs that AI will cause to decline
Downsizing workforce where AI can replicate people's work
Organization has low exposure to AI

aiSpecificStrategies.keyFinding: Reskilling and upskilling the existing workforce is the top AI-specific workforce strategy, planned by 77% of employers across 45 of 55 economies.

trainingNeeds.description: If the world's workforce was made up of 100 people, 59 would need training by 2030. Of these, 29 could be upskilled in their current roles, 19 redeployed, and 11 would be unlikely to receive the needed reskilling.

humanMachineFrontier.description: The proportional share of tasks performed solely by humans is expected to decline from 47% to 33% by 2030, while technology-performed tasks rise from 22% to 34%.

aiSkillDemandByIndustry.note: Share of employers reporting increasing need for AI and big data skills by 2030, by industry.

investmentContext.finding: Since the release of ChatGPT in November 2022, investment flows into AI have increased nearly eightfold.
investmentContext.genAIAdoption: GenAI considerably reduces barriers to usage and the need for specialized technical knowledge through natural language processing.
